steps.title: Use the common-use clue
steps.content: The passage contrasts expert advice to create complicated passwords with users choosing convenience. The example that "password" was the second most commonly used password points to an approach many people use.

steps.title: Match the frequency word
steps.content: Choice A contradicts the uncreative example. Choice B is too vague; usefulness is not the point. Choice D contradicts the simple, convenient password example. The needed word means widely adopted or common. The answer is C.
